title: ICE Detention Facility Solicitation Records — Surprise AZ

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, 5 U.S.C. § 552, I request the following records:
All documents related to solicitation number 70CDCR26R00000008, issued by the U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement Detention Compliance and Removals office, including but not limited to:

The complete solicitation package
Statement of Work or Performance Work Statement
Any pre-solicitation communications or market research documents
Requirements development documentation
Any amendments or modifications to the solicitation

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Donovan McLaughlin

title: EscambiaVotes GIS Mapping Data & IT Administration (2022-Present)

communications.subject: Florida Sunshine Law Request: EscambiaVotes GIS Mapping Data & IT Administration (2022-Present)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Florida Sunshine Law, I hereby request the following records:

To the Public Records Custodian / Hon. Robert Bender, Escambia County Supervisor of Elections:

Under the Florida Sunshine Law (Chapter 119, F.S.), I am requesting access to and copies of the following public records regarding the public-facing election results portal hosted on or linked via EscambiaVotes.gov.

Publicly available data on the EscambiaVotes portal indicates a systemic, multi-year degradation of the GIS precinct mapping module. Specifically, the public map functioned correctly during the 2022 General Election (last updated 12/8/2022). However, it suffered a complete failure during the 2024 Primary and General Elections (displaying no map data), and remains only partially functional as of the 2025 Special Election (displaying broken or unlinked shapefiles).

Because the underlying tabular voting data is actively present on the site, this request seeks to audit the administrative failure of the GIS mapping API and the visual transparency tool itself. I am requesting the following records:

1. IT and Administrative Correspondence (Dec 2022 - Present):
Any internal IT helpdesk tickets, error logs, or administrative memorandums generated between December 8, 2022, and the present date regarding GIS shapefile errors, precinct redistricting desynchronization, or the failure of the mapping API to visually populate precinct data on the public portal.

2. Third-Party Vendor Communications:
Any communications (including emails, letters, or service requests) between the Escambia County Supervisor of Elections office and the third-party election software vendor/portal host (e.g., VR Systems) dated between December 8, 2022, and the present date regarding the broken map data, API error resolution, or portal maintenance for the 2024 and 2025 elections.

Statutory Compliance:
If you determine that any portion of these requested records is exempt from disclosure under Chapter 119, Florida law requires you to state in writing the basis for the exemption, including the exact statutory citation authorizing the redaction or denial.

Please advise me of any costs associated with fulfilling this request prior to processing.

Thank you for your lawful compliance.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Matthew Joseph Luecht

title: Taylor’s Lawn Service Contractor Performance Records

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Tennessee Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

I am requesting records related to Taylor’s Lawn Service for the following contracts: #92296, #93874, #94082, #99573, #99575, #99576, #99577, #99578, and #105471.

Specifically, I am requesting any records reflecting performance issues, including cure or default notices, withheld payments, invoice deductions, disputed charges, and any written correspondence regarding deficiencies or service quality.

For purposes of identifying responsive records, this request includes communications containing the following terms: complain OR "corrective action" OR deficient OR missed OR "not complete" OR redo OR "re-do" OR rework OR unsatisfactory OR problem OR issue OR bad AND ("Taylor’s Lawn Service" OR "Taylors Lawn Service" OR "@taylors").

Please provide records in electronic format.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 7 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Kent Hoover, a citizen of Tennessee, in coordination with Drek Hawkins.
